title: GTA VI Is Finally Here. Don't Expect to Pay Less Than $80
gist: Grand Theft Auto VI launches November 19 at $80 for the standard edition—the highest base price ever for a major game—with a $100 ultimate edition, signaling a new pricing tier for AAA gaming. GTA V generated $1 billion in its first three days and shipped 230 million copies across its lifecycle, giving Take-Two Interactive an extraordinary performance benchmark that GTA VI must clear.

insights.claim: GTA VI is priced at $80 for the standard edition and $100 for the ultimate edition on launch, setting the highest base price ever for a major video game title.
insights.speaker: Boone Ashworth
insights.so_what: If GTA VI succeeds at $80—a $10 increase over recent AAA norms—it could establish a new industry price floor, benefiting publishers across the sector.
insights.quote: GTA VI will set players back $80 for the standard edition or $100 for the ultimate edition

insights.claim: NYU Stern professor Joost van Dreunen argues that GTA VI's $80 price point will not deter fans because GTA V demonstrated that Rockstar commands exceptional consumer loyalty.
insights.speaker: Joost van Dreunen
insights.so_what: Academic endorsement of price inelasticity for franchise titles gives investors cover to model aggressive revenue assumptions for GTA VI at launch.

insights.claim: GTA V earned $1 billion in revenue within its first three days of release and has shipped approximately 230 million copies since its 2013 launch, generating an estimated $10 billion in total revenue including GTA Online.
insights.speaker: Boone Ashworth
insights.so_what: GTA V's $10B lifecycle performance creates an extraordinary earnings bar; GTA VI launching at higher price and with expanded online ecosystem could exceed it over time, representing a multi-year revenue event for Take-Two.
insights.quote: GTA V sold $1 billion worth of copies in its first three days and has shipped approximately 230 million copies

insights.claim: Rockstar Games invested more than $1 billion in developing GTA VI, making it among the most expensive entertainment productions in history.
insights.speaker: Boone Ashworth
insights.so_what: A $1B+ production budget means GTA VI must achieve blockbuster commercial performance to justify investment; this creates high-stakes launch pressure for Take-Two's stock.

insights.claim: Rockstar acquired Cfx, the operator of a popular GTA modding marketplace, in 2023, consolidating control over the GTA modding ecosystem ahead of GTA VI's launch.
insights.speaker: Boone Ashworth
insights.so_what: Acquiring the modding platform could either help Rockstar monetize community content or generate friction if the acquisition is seen as restricting the mod ecosystem that sustained GTA V's long tail.

insights.claim: GTA VI preorders opened on the same day as the article's publication, following the November 19 release date announcement, with the game positioned as the defining gaming event of 2026.
insights.speaker: Boone Ashworth
insights.so_what: Preorder opening is a trackable demand signal; strong early preorder numbers would validate the $80 price tier and give Take-Two a forward revenue indicator before launch.
